content:
# Numbers Chapter 33 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 190-192 | **Source Image:** `NUM_033_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_033_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_033_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0139-pa_0140

**1.** These are the journeys of the sons of Israel who went out from the land of Egypt according to their hosts, by the hand of Moses and Aaron.

**2.** And Moses wrote down their starting points according to their journeys by the command of YHWH; and these are their journeys according to their starting points.

**3.** And they set out from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month — on the day after the Passover the sons of Israel went out with a high hand in the eyes of all Egypt,

**4.** while the Egyptians were burying those whom YHWH had struck down among them, every firstborn; and upon their gods YHWH executed judgments.

**5.** And the sons of Israel set out from Rameses and camped at Succoth.

**6.** And they set out from Succoth and camped at Etham, which is on the edge of the wilderness.

**7.** And they set out from Etham and turned back to Pi-hahiroth, which is before Baal-zephon, and they camped before Migdol.

**8.** And they set out from before Hahiroth and crossed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness, and they went a journey of three days in the wilderness of Etham and camped at Marah.

**9.** And they set out from Marah and came to Elim; and in Elim were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees, and they camped there.

**10.** And they set out from Elim and camped by the Sea of Reeds.

**11.** And they set out from the Sea of Reeds and camped in the wilderness of Sin.

**12.** And they set out from the wilderness of Sin and camped at Dophkah.

**13.** And they set out from Dophkah and camped at Alush.

**14.** And they set out from Alush and camped at Rephidim; and there was no water there for the people to drink.

**15.** And they set out from Rephidim and camped in the wilderness of Sinai.

**16.** And they set out from the wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.

**17.** And they set out from Kibroth-hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.

**18.** And they set out from Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah.

**19.** And they set out from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-perez.

**20.** And they set out from Rimmon-perez and camped at Libnah.

**21.** And they set out from Libnah and camped at Rissah.

**22.** And they set out from Rissah and camped at Kehelathah.

**23.** And they set out from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher.

**24.** And they set out from Mount Shepher and camped at Haradah.

**25.** And they set out from Haradah and camped at Makheloth.

**26.** And they set out from Makheloth and camped at Tahath.

**27.** And they set out from Tahath and camped at Terah.

**28.** And they set out from Terah and camped at Mithkah.

**29.** And they set out from Mithkah and camped at Hashmonah.

**30.** And they set out from Hashmonah and camped at Moseroth.

**31.** And they set out from Moseroth and camped at Bene-jaakan.

**32.** And they set out from Bene-jaakan and camped at Hor-haggidgad.

**33.** And they set out from Hor-haggidgad and camped at Jotbathah.

**34.** And they set out from Jotbathah and camped at Abronah.

**35.** And they set out from Abronah and camped at Ezion-geber.

**36.** And they set out from Ezion-geber and camped in the wilderness of Zin, that is, Kadesh.

**37.** And they set out from Kadesh and camped at Mount Hor, on the edge of the land of Edom.

**38.** And Aaron the priest went up to Mount Hor at the command of YHWH and died there, in the fortieth year after the sons of Israel had gone out from the land of Egypt, in the fifth month, on the first day of the month.

**39.** And Aaron was one hundred and twenty-three years old when he died on Mount Hor.

**40.** And the Canaanite, the king of Arad — and he was dwelling in the Negev in the land of Canaan — heard of the coming of the sons of Israel.

**41.** And they set out from Mount Hor and camped at Zalmonah.

**42.** And they set out from Zalmonah and camped at Punon.

**43.** And they set out from Punon and camped at Oboth.

**44.** And they set out from Oboth and camped at Iye-abarim, on the border of Moab.

**45.** And they set out from Iyim and camped at Dibon-gad.

**46.** And they set out from Dibon-gad and camped at Almon-diblathaim.

**47.** And they set out from Almon-diblathaim and camped in the mountains of Abarim, before Nebo.

**48.** And they set out from the mountains of Abarim and camped in the plains of Moab, by the Jordan at Jericho.

**49.** And they camped by the Jordan, from Beth-jeshimoth as far as Abel-shittim, in the plains of Moab.

**50.** And YHWH spoke to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho, saying,

**51.** "Speak to the sons of Israel and say to them: When you cross over the Jordan into the land of Canaan,

**52.** then you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land from before you, and you shall destroy all their carved stones, and all their cast images you shall destroy, and all their high places you shall demolish.

**53.** And you shall dispossess the inhabitants of the land and dwell in it, for to you I have given the land to possess it.

**54.** And you shall apportion the land by lot according to your clans. To the larger you shall give a larger inheritance, and to the smaller you shall give a smaller inheritance. Wherever the lot falls for each one, there it shall be his. According to the tribes of your fathers you shall receive an inheritance.

**55.** But if you do not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you, then those whom you allow to remain of them shall be as barbs in your eyes and as thorns in your sides, and they shall trouble you in the land in which you are dwelling.

**56.** And it shall be that as I intended to do to them, I shall do to you."

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Textual Notes
- **v.1** Heb. *masa'ehem* (מַסְעֵיהֶם) — "journeys/stages," from the root נסע (to pull up, set out). The noun denotes the individual stages or stations of a journey.
- **v.2** The verse contains a deliberate chiastic reversal: "starting points according to their journeys" / "journeys according to their starting points" (מוֹצָאֵיהֶם לְמַסְעֵיהֶם / מַסְעֵיהֶם לְמוֹצָאֵיהֶם).
- **v.3** Heb. *beyad ramah* (בְּיָד רָמָה) — lit. "with a high hand," meaning boldly, defiantly, without fear.
- **v.4** Heb. *ubeiloheihem* (וּבֵאלֹהֵיהֶם) — "and upon their gods." The LXX (Vaticanus) reads καὶ ἐν τοῖς εἰδώλοις αὐτῶν ("and upon their idols"), interpreting *elohim* as idol-images rather than deities.
- **v.7** The LC reads פְּנֵי הַחִירֹת (Pene Hahiroth / Pi-hahiroth). The place-name is spelled slightly differently from Exod 14:2 (פִּי הַחִירֹת).
- **v.8** Heb. *mipnei hahiroth* — "from before Hahiroth." The MT shortens the place name here compared to v.7. LXX harmonizes with the fuller form.
- **v.9** The detail of "twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees" echoes Exod 15:27 verbatim.
- **v.38** Heb. *al pi YHWH* (עַל־פִּי יהוה) — lit. "at the mouth of YHWH," i.e., at YHWH's command. Rabbinic tradition interprets Aaron's death "by the mouth of YHWH" as death by divine kiss.
- **v.39** Aaron's age: one hundred and twenty-three years (שָׁלֹשׁ וְעֶשְׂרִים וּמְאַת שָׁנָה). The LC is clear on this figure.
- **v.44** Heb. *Iye ha-Abarim* (עִיֵּי הָעֲבָרִים) — "ruins of the Abarim," i.e., Iye-abarim. The abbreviated form *Iyim* (עִיִּים) appears in v.45.
- **v.52** Three distinct terms for pagan cult objects: (1) *maskiyyotam* (מַשְׂכִּיֹּתָם) — "carved stones" or "figured stones" (possibly stone reliefs or engraved images); (2) *tsalmei massekhotam* (צַלְמֵי מַסֵּכֹתָם) — "cast images" (molten metal idols); (3) *bamotam* (בָּמוֹתָם) — "high places" (elevated cult sites).
- **v.54** Heb. *begoral* (בְּגוֹרָל) — "by lot." The apportioning of the land is to be determined by sacred lot, not by human choice.
- **v.55** Heb. *lesikkim be'eineikhem* (לְשִׂכִּים בְּעֵינֵיכֶם) — "as barbs in your eyes"; *velitsninim betsiddeikhem* (וְלִצְנִינִם בְּצִדֵּיכֶם) — "as thorns in your sides." Vivid imagery for persistent irritation and affliction.
- **v.56** The final warning: what YHWH intended to do to the Canaanites he will do to Israel if they fail to obey. This creates the theological frame for the entire conquest narrative.

### LXX Variants (Vaticanus)
- **v.4** LXX adds "all" (πάντα) before "firstborn," emphasizing the totality of the plague.
- **v.7** LXX reads "which is opposite Baal-zephon" and specifies the camp location "opposite Magdol" (ἀπέναντι Μαγδώλου), consistent with the MT.
- **v.8** LXX has "the wilderness of three days" (ὁδὸν τριῶν ἡμερῶν), matching the MT precisely.
- **v.40** LXX preserves the parenthetical note about the Canaanite king of Arad dwelling in the Negev, consistent with the LC text.
- **v.52** LXX renders *maskiyyotam* as τὰς σκοπιὰς αὐτῶν ("their lookout posts/watchtowers"), a notably different interpretation from the MT's "carved stones."
- **v.55** LXX renders the metaphors as σκόλοπες ἐν τοῖς ὀφθαλμοῖς ὑμῶν ("stakes in your eyes") and βολίδες ἐν ταῖς πλευραῖς ὑμῶν ("darts in your sides"), intensifying the imagery.

### Structural Note
Numbers 33 divides into two distinct literary units: (1) The travel itinerary (vv.1-49), a unique genre in the Torah functioning as a retrospective summary of the entire wilderness period, listing forty-two stations from Rameses to the plains of Moab; and (2) YHWH's command concerning the conquest of Canaan (vv.50-56), which serves as a transition to the land-division instructions that follow in chapters 34-36. The itinerary section has a highly formulaic structure ("And they set out from X and camped at Y") that emphasizes the completeness and divine ordering of Israel's journey.
